facts.context: When both a dominant and recessive gene are present, which of the following is true?
facts.truth: The dominant trait will be visible.
distractors: The recessive trait will be visible.
It cannot be predicted which trait will be visible.

facts.context: A student places a ball on the ground and kicks it. The ball moves along the ground. Why does the ball move?
facts.truth: The kick applies a contact force to the ball.
distractors: The kick decreases the weight of the ball.
The kick decreases the force of gravity acting on the ball.
The kick removes friction between the ball and the ground.

facts.context: What is a primary role of decomposers in an ecosystem?
facts.truth: They cycle matter by returning carbon and other matter to the ecosystem.
distractors: They eliminate matter by taking nitrogen from the ecosystem.
They eliminate matter by taking phosphorus from the ecosystem.
They cycle matter by returning oxygen and other matter to the ecosystem.

distractors: to keep the electrical wire warm
to make the electrical wire stronger
to make the electrical wire more flexible

facts.context: According to the endosymbiotic theory, the outer membrane of the chloroplast originated from which organelle?
facts.truth: the cell membrane of an eukaryotic host cell
distractors: the lysosome of an engulfed prokaryotic cell
the chloroplast of an engulfed prokaryotic cell
the chloroplast of an eukaryotic host cell

facts.context: Which information would probably be most helpful to someone trying to identify a mineral sample?
facts.truth: hardness and streak of the sample
distractors: location and mass of the sample
shape and texture of the sample
color and size of the sample

James placed a breath mint and a lump of toothpaste in a cup of water and let it sit overnight. Which statement best explains why the breath mint was gone the following morning, but the toothpaste was still at the bottom of the cup?
facts.truth: The breath mint was more soluble than the toothpaste.
distractors: The breath mint was less soluble than the toothpaste.
The breath mint had a lower density than the toothpaste.
The breath mint had a greater density than the toothpaste.

facts.context: Many scientists are convinced that an increase of certain gases in the atmosphere is affecting global temperatures. What makes this hypothesis difficult to prove?
distractors: Many human activities produce these gases.

facts.truth: minimizing the need for manual labor.
distractors: maximizing the price of consumer goods.
maximizing the use of non-renewable resources.

facts.context: Hawaii is composed mainly of volcanoes with gentle slopes and large surface areas. Which characteristics best describe the lava these volcanoes have released?

facts.truth: a hard metal case with flexible foam lining
distractors: a flexible metal case with smooth plastic lining
a strong wooden case with lightweight plastic lining
a lightweight wooden case with strong aluminum lining
